subject: Human Resources
title: Market Pricing and Benchmarking
description: A 60-second lesson on Market Pricing and Benchmarking.

hook.text: A company pays its data scientists $90K. The market rate is $140K. They can't figure out why nobody stays.

buildup.text: Market pricing compares internal salaries to external market data—salary surveys from providers like Radford or Mercer.

discovery.text: Companies choose a market position: lead (pay above market), match (pay at market), or lag (pay below).

twist.text: Market data has limits—it reflects what companies pay, not what roles are worth. Hot skills inflate faster than surveys can track.

climax.text: Regular benchmarking—at least annually—prevents pay from drifting out of competitiveness as markets shift.

punchline.text: Know the market rate. Then decide where you want to stand.

quiz.question: What does 'market match' strategy mean?
options: Paying at the same level as the external market
Paying below market to save costs
